title: Crispy Garlic Jamaican Stir-fry
description: A light and crispy dish with a bold garlicky kick and the distinct flavors of ras el hanout, perfect for those on a Paleo diet.

ingredients.name: Ras el hanout spice blend
ingredients.notes: For flavoring the pork and creating a distinctive taste

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Minced finely to enhance the garlic flavor

ingredients.name: Paleo-friendly vegetable stock or water
ingredients.notes: Used for making a light sauce to drizzle over the dish

ingredients.name: Paleo-friendly olive oil or coconut oil
ingredients.notes: Used for stir-frying the ingredients and making a crispy texture

instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine the pork slices with ras el hanout spice blend. Mix well to ensure even coating.
Place the mixture in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to marinate and allow flavors to infuse into the meat.
Preheat your wok or skillet over medium-high heat until very hot (around 160°C/320°F). Add oil, then add the garlic cloves.
Add the marinated pork slices to the pan. Cook for about 5 minutes on each side, until golden and crispy but not dry. Remove from heat immediately after cooking.
Combine the paleo-friendly soy sauce or tamari with the water in a small bowl. Heat over medium-high flame until hot. Pour this mixture into the wok with the cooked pork.
Stir-fry for about 2 minutes, allowing the liquid to slightly reduce and thicken slightly. If needed, add more water or stock to adjust consistency.
Fold in the lightly toasted sesame seeds before serving. Drizzle over a paleo-friendly sauce (made from light vegetable stock) if desired.
